response.justification: Candidate has strong distributed-systems and full-stack engineering foundations with current involvement in agentic AI infrastructure, but lacks the critical hard requirements: no demonstrated fintech or financial-services domain experience (credit systems, payment flows, regulatory compliance), no proven track record operating production multi-agent AI systems in high-stakes environments, and unclear evidence of scaling engineering teams to the 10-30+ person range required for executive leadership. The current MCP and agentic-framework work is encouraging but presented too vaguely to offset missing fintech and team-building credentials.
strengths: Current role explicitly involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, showing recent hands-on work with agentic frameworks.
Strong foundation in distributed systems, event-driven architecture, microservices, and real-time low-latency systems (millions of HTTP requests/second, asynchronous I/O).
Demonstrated technical leadership through multiple Staff engineer roles, open-source framework creation, and teaching initiatives (workshops, courses, mentoring).
concerns: No fintech or financial-services experience despite it being an explicit hard requirement—no evidence of credit systems, payment flows, trading, regulatory compliance, or risk assessment work.

Production agentic AI systems experience not demonstrated; current MCP work appears to be developer-tooling focused rather than multi-agent decision systems in regulated or high-stakes commercial contexts.
Team scaling unclear—resume lists multiple senior/staff titles but does not evidence building and scaling engineering organizations from 10 to 30+ people or developing technical leadership layers.
